params.name: Pt-Cont Transistor
params.description:
On May 12, 1947 AD, Shockley developed a Point-Cont Transistor. An invention from Shockely, Bardeen, and Brattain. The precursor to the micro-evolution revolution. With a small, low-power electron device replacing the bulky, high-power electron tube, it laid the foundation for a generation of ICs, microprocessers, and computer memory, thanks to the emergence of transistors. [Pros] Small: Light weight helps the miniaturization of electron devices
Low voltage: It can use batteries
No warm …
